message:
feat(tests,storage,sync): add workspace failure fixtures, dataset registry, and harden concurrency tests

Add comprehensive workspace failure test fixtures for corrupt DBs, JSONL
conflict markers, sidecar mismatches, and interrupted rebuilds. Expand
e2e concurrency coverage with dataset_registry module and improve SQLite
storage robustness with better lock timeout handling and sync module
enhancements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -503,12 +503,12 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","title":"Encode an executable workspace health contract and invariant matrix","description":"## Purpose
Turn the taxonomy into an executable health contract so code and tests can assert specific invariants instead of guessing.

## Cover
- Required agreements among SQLite, JSONL, metadata, and sidecars.
- What counts as recoverable vs unrecoverable.
- Which derived tables may be rebuilt and which source-of-truth tables may not be silently discarded.
- What `doctor`, startup/open, write recovery, and sync status must each report for the same workspace.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invariants are specific enough to encode in tests and diagnostics.
- The contract explicitly names acceptable automatic recovery actions and forbidden silent behaviors.
- Derived-state rebuild rules are separated from primary-data repair rules.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:24.216654762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:04.925232727Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:04.912510332Z","close_reason":"Documented the executable workspace health contract and invariant matrix in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md so later diagnostics/tests can assert the same repair and reporting rules.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","invariants","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:24.216654762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:46.665559578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":190,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reasoning: the project already has implicit contracts spread across open/recovery/sync/doctor paths. This bead makes them explicit so later work can fail fast when one surface says 'healthy' and another surface proves otherwise.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25Z"},{"id":216,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CreamBasin continuing on the next unblocked bead. I validated that the failure-taxonomy content landed on the troubleshooting surface, so I am taking this follow-on task to encode the explicit workspace health contract and invariant matrix in the architecture docs.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:19:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","title":"Define the incident bundle capture workflow for field failures","description":"## Purpose
Define the minimal reproducibility bundle we should gather the first time a field failure is reported so we stop losing crucial evidence.

## Bundle Contents
- failing command + stdout/stderr
- `br doctor --json`
- `metadata.json`
- `issues.jsonl`
- presence and hashes of `beads.db`, `beads.db-wal`, `beads.db-shm`, `beads.db-journal`
- environment overrides that affect discovery/path/output
- whether multiple agents/processes were active

## Acceptance Criteria
- The bundle is small enough to request quickly during incident response.
- The captured data is sufficient to classify the failure using the taxonomy from this track.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:54.847666702Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:54.834341839Z","close_reason":"Defined the minimal incident evidence bundle for field failures in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident-response","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:55.594416915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because ad hoc bug reports waste time and collapse into speculation. If we cannot preserve the evidence bundle, we cannot expect to reproduce or eliminate field failures systematically.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","title":"Build a sanitized real-world workspace failure corpus and replay harness","description":"## Why
Hermetic tempdir tests are not enough. We need a standing corpus of realistic damaged or drifted workspaces and a way to replay them automatically.

## Scope
Create sanitized fixtures that model the important bad states and wire them into a replay harness that exercises open/read/write/doctor/sync behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The corpus includes the representative classes from the taxonomy.
- The replay harness can be run locally and in CI.
- Failures in the corpus are easy to inspect and update intentionally.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863684637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","reliability","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863085696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track converts anecdotal production pain into durable regression assets. The goal is not synthetic benchmark data; it is realistic workspace states that future code must handle safely.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:28Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","title":"Create a sanitized fixture corpus for representative corrupt and drifted workspaces","description":"## Purpose
Build a curated, sanitized fixture corpus for representative workspace failure states.

## Required Fixture Families
- corrupt or partially corrupt `beads.db`
- `beads.db` + stale/missing `-wal`/`-shm` combinations
- malformed schema / duplicate schema-entry states
- JSONL with malformed lines or conflict markers
- DB/JSONL/metadata disagreement
- path/env discovery edge cases that only appear outside hermetic tests
- interrupted rebuild/sync/export leftovers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fixtures are sanitized and safe to store in-repo.
- Each fixture includes metadata explaining the intended state and expected classification.
- The corpus is organized so future incidents can add new fixtures instead of spawning one-off repro scripts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:29.035540341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:04.515757803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corruption","fixtures","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:04.515270731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:29.035540341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Key consideration: a fixture corpus only helps if it preserves the interesting structure of the failure. Do not over-normalize fixtures into clean states that no longer reproduce the real anomaly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30Z"}]}

Do not over-normalize fixtures into clean states that no longer reproduce the real anomaly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30Z"},{"id":220,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming this now. Initial focus: inventory existing malformed/corrupt workspace fixtures and repro directories already in-repo, identify the canonical fixture layout under tests/fixtures or adjacent harness code, then add a sanitized metadata-backed corpus that downstream reliability beads can consume.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:33:27Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed fixture corpus setup under tests/fixtures/workspace_failures/. Added seven small, metadata-backed workspace cases covering corrupt DB text, JSONL conflict markers, DB/JSONL drift, duplicate config rows, metadata-driven custom paths, WAL-without-SHM sidecars, and interrupted rebuild leftovers. Added loader/support APIs in tests/common/dataset_registry.rs so tests can enumerate fixtures and materialize a transport-safe visible beads/ payload back into runtime .beads/ workspaces. Verification: cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- cargo test workspace_failure_fixture -- --nocapture; rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets; rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-03-12T12:07:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","title":"Add a replay harness that asserts command behavior against the fixture corpus","description":"## Purpose
Add a harness that mounts each fixture workspace and asserts the expected behavior across the main operational surfaces.

## Required Surfaces
- startup/open
- read commands (`list`, `show`, `ready`, `where`, `info`)
- write commands (`create`, `update`, `close`, `reopen`, `label`, `comments`, `delete` as appropriate)
- `doctor`
- `sync --status`, import/export/rebuild behavior where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each fixture records expected classification and expected command outcomes.
- The harness distinguishes: healthy, degraded-but-usable, recoverable, and unsafe.
- Regressions are reported in a way that makes the failing fixture/state obvious.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:22.350537886Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reliability","replay","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:22.349991073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:13.418006391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is the bridge between plan-space and code-space. Once this bead lands, future corruption fixes should start by adding or updating a fixture expectation instead of inventing bespoke local repro steps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","title":"Generate deterministic long-lived workspace evolution scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Create long-lived workspace scenarios that accumulate real history over time rather than relying only on one-shot fresh temp workspaces.

## Cover
- many commands over one workspace
- restarts between commands
- mixed auto-import/auto-flush behavior
- repeated derived-state rebuilds
- state after partial failures and subsequent recovery attempts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenario generator can produce deterministic seeds for reproducible failures.
- Generated workspaces can be fed back into the fixture/replay system.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:40.460091148Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","state-drift"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:31.233437625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:40.459575482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: production users do not reinitialize  for every command. Long-lived state accumulation is where 'suite is green but field is broken' bugs are born.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","title":"Expand crash, concurrency, and filesystem realism coverage","description":"## Why
The current concurrency coverage proves some lock semantics, but it does not yet model real agent swarms, crash timing, filesystem failures, or prolonged mixed usage on one workspace.

## Scope
Expand destructive realism: crash injection, multi-process contention, filesystem anomalies, and long-running stress.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reliability tests model the kinds of failures users actually trigger accidentally.
- We can answer "what happens if the process dies here?" for the critical write/sync phases.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:34.087986025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:50.935545188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:34.087986025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:50.934904899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track intent: move from 'SQLite locking works under a narrow benchmark' to 'the workspace survives hostile real-world timing and storage conditions.'","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","title":"Add a crash-injection matrix for all critical mutation and sync phases","description":"## Purpose
Add crash-injection coverage across the critical mutation and sync phases.

## Required Operations
- create
- update
- close/reopen/defer/undefer
- delete
- label/comment mutation
- sync import/export/rebuild/recovery paths

## Required Crash Points
- before primary write
- after primary write but before derived-state rebuild
- during temp-file write
- before/after rename
- before dirty-flag clear
- during/after backup/quarantine creation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each crash point has explicit expected post-restart behavior.
- No crash leaves the workspace silently misclassified as healthy.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.412355354Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash-consistency","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:49.444077784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.411766251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":197,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note: this bead is about interruption semantics, not throughput. Prefer fewer operations with well-defined crash points over broad but shallow coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","title":"Broaden multi-process and mixed-agent contention scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Expand concurrency coverage from narrow lock-timeout scenarios to realistic multi-process and mixed-agent usage.

## Cover
- concurrent writers on one workspace
- read/write races across commands
- multiple agents issuing different command families interleaved
- routed/external workspace access during local mutation
- background processes touching the same `.beads` directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests define which outcomes are acceptable vs unacceptable.
- Contention results in bounded failure/retry behavior, not drift or corruption.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:07:24.427548028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:09.543615058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":198,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Important consideration: success here does not mean every race magically succeeds. It means failures are explicit, bounded, and non-corrupting.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39Z"},{"id":218,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Working this now in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Scope: add coverage for interleaved command families on one workspace, plus external BEADS_DIR/status access against the same workspace during local mutation, with final integrity assertions to distinguish acceptable lock contention from corruption/drift.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:07:24Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","title":"Broaden multi-process and mixed-agent contention scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Expand concurrency coverage from narrow lock-timeout scenarios to realistic multi-process and mixed-agent usage.

## Cover
- concurrent writers on one workspace
- read/write races across commands
- multiple agents issuing different command families interleaved
- routed/external workspace access during local mutation
- background processes touching the same `.beads` directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests define which outcomes are acceptable vs unacceptable.
- Contention results in bounded failure/retry behavior, not drift or corruption.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47.309252200Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:29.228335520Z","close_reason":"Expanded bounded-failure concurrency coverage and validated integrity invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:09.543615058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":198,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Important consideration: success here does not mean every race magically succeeds. It means failures are explicit, bounded, and non-corrupting.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39Z"},{"id":218,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Working this now in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Scope: add coverage for interleaved command families on one workspace, plus external BEADS_DIR/status access against the same workspace during local mutation, with final integrity assertions to distinguish acceptable lock contention from corruption/drift.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:07:24Z"},{"id":219,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Added env-clearing/env-aware helper coverage, broadened contention assertions to bounded explicit failures vs corruption, added interleaved-command-family and external-BEADS_DIR/background-status scenarios, and tightened existing concurrency cases to assert final integrity based on successful operations. Verification: rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:44Z"},{"id":221,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added broader contention coverage in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs for mixed command families, routed/external access, background sync status checks, and actor-oriented mutation scenarios. Also stabilized e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry with an explicit lock timeout for deterministic remote verification. Verified with rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, and cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","title":"Model filesystem realism and external interference scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Model filesystem and storage anomalies that hermetic tempdir tests almost never hit.

## Cover
- sidecar mismatch and disappearance
- rename failure / interrupted atomic export
- disk-full or temp-file allocation failure
- permissions changes
- external sync/editor/cloud-drive interference on `.beads`
- unusual HOME/path/env interactions that affect discovery and storage location

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests assert data preservation and honest diagnostics.
- We understand which anomalies are tolerated, which trigger repair, and which require operator intervention.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:19.121815354Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filesystem","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:19.121325297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is where lab conditions diverge sharply from the field. If a scenario only happens on a user's real filesystem, the current tempdir-first suite is likely blind to it.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","title":"Add a long-running single-workspace stress suite","description":"## Purpose
Create a long-running stress suite that reuses one workspace across many commands and processes to surface drift, stale assumptions, and accumulated-state bugs.

## Cover
- repeated issue lifecycle operations
- repeated sync/import/export status checks
- intermittent failures and restarts
- mixed human-style and agent-style command ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- The suite can run for a meaningful duration without manual babysitting.
- Failures leave behind enough artifacts to classify and reproduce.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:37.660222051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:28.600633433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:37.659700575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because the product is a stateful CLI, not a stateless request handler. Bugs that require dozens or hundreds of operations on the same workspace will never show up in per-test fresh-init patterns.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","title":"Harden detection, recovery, and no-data-loss guarantees","description":"## Why
Even with better tests, the product must behave safely when it encounters a damaged or inconsistent workspace in production. Detection, recovery, and operator messaging need to be consistent and trustworthy.

## Scope
Unify anomaly classification, preserve evidence before repair, verify post-recovery state, and make `doctor`/startup/write behavior agree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Automatic recovery is explicit, reversible, and validated.
- We do not report "OK" for workspaces that are only partially readable or that fail on writes.
- Recovery logic never silently hides potential data loss.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.162394901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:59.944386891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.161785269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track is where field pain becomes product behavior. The product must either operate safely, recover safely, or stop with actionable evidence; the current gray zone in between is unacceptable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:45Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+# Workspace Failure Fixtures
+
+This corpus stores small, sanitized workspace roots for reliability regression work.
+
+Each fixture directory contains:
+
+- `fixture.json`: human-readable metadata about the failure family and why it matters
+- `beads/`: the workspace payload that the loader materializes into `.beads/` inside an isolated test root
+
+Conventions:
+
+- Every fixture is a complete workspace root, not just a raw `.beads` dump.
+- The checked-in payload lives under visible `beads/` because the remote `rch` transport used for cargo test/check/clippy does not preserve newly added hidden untracked directories reliably.
+- Sidecars, recovery artifacts, and other debris are preserved when they are the point of the case.
+- The payloads are intentionally small so they can live in git and be inspected by hand.
+- New fixtures should model one primary anomaly each. If a future incident needs a new combination, add a new directory instead of mutating an unrelated case.
+
+Current families covered here:
+
+- corrupt or non-SQLite `beads.db`
+- JSONL conflict-marker corruption
+- DB/JSONL disagreement
+- duplicate config rows after legacy-schema drift
+- metadata-based custom path discovery
+- WAL sidecar without matching SHM
+- interrupted rebuild leftovers and recovery debris

+*.db
+*.db-journal
+*.db-shm

+

+

+*.tmp
+
+# Local history backups
+.br_history/

+.sync.lock

+

+

+daemon.pid
+bd.sock
+sync-state.json
+

+# bv (beads viewer) lock file

+  "notes": [

+    "Intended to drive rebuild-or-bypass behavior rather than low-level SQLite recovery."
+  ]
+}

+{
+  "name": "db_jsonl_disagreement",
+  "family": "drift",
+  "description": "The database and JSONL export disagree because JSONL contains an extra issue record that was never imported into the database.",
+  "expected_classification": "degraded",
+  "expected_command_surface": [
+    "startup/open",
+    "doctor",
+    "sync --status",
+    "sync --import-only"
+  ],
+  "source_hint": "Models the DB/JSONL mismatch class called out in beads_rust-rsei.2.1.",
+  "notes": [
+    "Useful for future replay tests that need to distinguish stale export drift from hard corruption.",
+    "The JSONL payload stays syntactically valid so classification depends on cross-store comparison."
+  ]
+}

+{
+  "name": "interrupted_rebuild_leftovers",
+  "family": "recovery_debris",
+  "description": "A failed rebuild left a bad replacement database in place along with preserved backups and recovery artifacts.",
+  "expected_classification": "recoverable",
+  "expected_command_surface": [
+    "startup/open",
+    "doctor --repair",
+    "sync --status",
+    "history"
+  ],
+  "source_hint": "Inspired by the large repo-root recovery dumps collected during real-world investigation.",
+  "notes": [
+    "The live beads.db is plain text on purpose; the interesting state is the preserved backup and .br_recovery trail.",
+    "Future replay tests can assert that recovery tooling prefers the preserved backups over the broken live file."
+  ]
+}

+{
+  "name": "jsonl_conflict_markers",
+  "family": "malformed_jsonl",
+  "description": "The JSONL export contains git merge conflict markers wrapped around otherwise valid records.",
+  "expected_classification": "unsafe",
+  "expected_command_surface": [
+    "startup/open",
+    "doctor",
+    "sync --status",
+    "sync --import-only"
+  ],
+  "source_hint": "Derived from the conflict-marker rejection tests in tests/e2e_sync_fuzz_edge_cases.rs.",
+  "notes": [
+    "This case is syntactically broken by design and should never be treated as silently importable.",
+    "The database remains healthy so the fixture can distinguish interchange failure from primary-store failure."
+  ]
+}

+  "name": "sidecar_wal_without_shm",
+  "family": "sidecar_mismatch",
+  "description": "The workspace includes a stray WAL sidecar without a matching SHM sidecar or coordinated writer state.",

+  "expected_command_surface": [

+    "doctor",

+  ],
